commands.review_summary:
The branch changes `src/infra/install-safe-path.test.ts` to probe directory-link support and run three symlink boundary tests on Windows using junction links.

PR surface: Tests +17. Total +17 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. for the test gap: current main and v2026.6.1 hard-skip the three symlink boundary cases on Windows, so the missing Windows coverage is visible from source.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair lane is needed; the branch is reviewable as-is and no concrete automated fix is identified.

target.title: test: make install-safe-path symlink tests compatible with Windows
target.body:
Replaces the hardcoded Windows skips in the install-safe-path test suite with a dynamic `canCreateDirectorySymlinks` capability check. If directory symlinking is supported by the OS environment, the tests execute.

Additionally, uses directory junctions (via the `junction` link type) on Windows when creating parent directories to ensure compatibility without Administrator privileges.

### Verified Windows Proof

The following test execution log shows the test run on Windows. All directory junction/symlink tests executed and passed successfully (24 passed):

```
 RUN  v4.1.7 C:/Users/ANIRUDDHA/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/openclaw

 ✓  infra  ../../src/infra/install-safe-path.test.ts (24 tests) 485ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  24 passed (24)
```
target.author: aniruddhaadak80
